§1.1 Inverse Functions — VISUAL-FRAME gate1 視覺稽核

2026-06-17 · Stage 2 工程稿視覺驗收 · 19 場景 1080p mock，17 內容幀逐幀稽核

流程：make.py --backend mock 19 場景 1080p → ffmpeg -sseof 抽每場景最末（最滿）幀 → Workflow fan-out（17 幀 × visual-frame-audit agent，對照 VISUAL-FRAME-RUBRIC V1–V8 blocking＋A1–A7 magnitude）→ 每個 blocking finding 派 3 個 refute-by-default skeptic 對抗式複驗（多數決）。

圖片均以 base64 內嵌（self-contained，換機 git clone 後仍可讀）。

結論：17 內容幀全審。raw V-blocking 1（三票複驗：confirmed 1、refuted 0）。 唯一 blocking＝recap point 04 caveat 出框（V1，3/3 票確認）已修 · 回歸 0 visual blocking。其餘 16 場景 V1–V8 全 clean，A 分均值 80–90。 使用者裁決後處理的 3 個 advisory：align_on＝false positive（不改）、label 顏色＝已修（template）、y=x label 壓線＝已修（per-deck），皆回歸 confirmed（見 §②）。其餘 A 面 polish nit 留待專門 pass。

① Blocking（V1，已修 → 回歸）

17 recap · V1 出框／裁切 · 3/3 skeptic 確認

修改前（caveat 被切）
修改後（重渲）
  • 證據：左欄 point 04「Find it: solve $y=f(x)$ for $x$, then swap $x$ and $y$ (check one-to-one first).」最後一行被切在畫面底部安全邊界外，丟失旁白強調的「先驗一對一」caveat（say:「…after checking one-to-one…」）。
  • 為何 Blocking：這是 recap 最末／最滿幀，所有 reveal 本應在場；關鍵教學資訊被裁出框＝V1 Blocking（非單純擁擠）。sizecheck 對 recap wrap 高度有已知盲區，故三閘未擋。
  • 修法（this-deck-only，與既有「recap 點縮短」慣例一致、不動共用模板）：把兩個 3 行長點縮成 2 行騰出垂直空間——point.0「different inputs give different outputs (horizontal line test)」→「distinct inputs give distinct outputs」；point.2「The inverse swaps…its graph reflects」→「Inverse swaps…graph reflects」。帶 caveat 的 point.3 逐字保留。
  • 回歸：獨立 visual-frame-audit agent 重判新 recap 幀：VERDICT 0 visual blocking。point 04「first).」完整落在底部安全邊界內（baseline ~478/540px、底下約 60px 間距）；全 4 點＋3 公式卡在框、無相撞；縮短 point.0／point.2 為 2 行未引入新 wrap／失衡／overlap，僅餘 low-severity A1／A4／A7 美學註記（右欄略偏下、卡片內距微差、recap 諸點刻意等重無單一焦點）。

② Advisory 處置（使用者裁決「先修 template bug」後）

gate1 的 A 面 advisory 未經三票對抗式複驗（只有 V-blocking 走複驗）。逐項調查後處置如下——其中 align_on 經查為 false positive，印證「未複驗的 A 面 advisory 不可盡信」。

①·a　derivation align_on:"=" — 調查後＝FALSE POSITIVE（不改）

audit 對 scene 14／16 報「align_on 宣告了卻渲成置中、= 沒對成一欄」。實測推翻：

  • 幾何量測（複現模板的實際 MathTex）：定位後每行 = 的 left-x 全等（+0.291），arrange+center 後仍全等（−0.593）＝完美一欄。
  • 放大裁切 scene 14 方程式區：5 行的第一個 = 確實對齊同一 x，各行左緣刻意錯開正是為了對齊 =。
  • 結論：VLM 把模板刻意的「整塊置中」誤讀成「= 沒對齊」。模板正常，不動（改它反而會弄壞正常對齊）。

①·b　graph_focus line label 顏色 ＋ ①·c　y=x label 壓線 — 已修＋回歸 confirmed

  • 顏色（template 根因）：graph_focus.py 的 line label 預設 role="warning"（紅）——故 color_role: muted 的 y=x 線、未設 label_role 時 label 掉成紅色。改為繼承該線的 color_role（與 function label 繼承曲線色一致）。blast radius 安全：唯二受影響的就是這兩條 y=x 線；demo 皆設顯式 label_role、不受影響。
  • 壓線（per-deck）：y=x label 的 label_point 挪離虛線（11 順手把與 cyan 曲線相疊的 y=x³ label 一起清開）。
  • 回歸：2 個獨立 visual-frame-audit agent 重判 scene 11／12＝各 0 visual blocking，兩修法 confirmed、無新碰撞。
11 修前（紅 label·壓線）
11 修後（muted·離線）
12 修前（紅 label·壓線）
12 修後（muted·離線）

④ 各場景幀＋findings

01 · can_we_run_it_backwards definition_math V clean

A1 88A2 82A3 90A4 90A5 88A6 78A7 84
4 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A6 · med] These two lines are full sentence-level teaching clauses (subject + 'each output comes from one input' / 'both map to 1/4'), yet they are centered. Per the typography note, sentence-level teaching prose should read left-aligned and only short title cards/eyebrows/captions may be centered. Centering teaching clauses creates a slightly ragged, slide-like read rather than an anchored left edge.
    → Left-align the two math/teaching lines to a shared left margin (aligned with the statement line's left edge at ~x=130) so the eye has a consistent left anchor; reserve centering for the title card and eyebrow only.
  • [A6 · low] The stacked \tfrac fractions render noticeably smaller than the surrounding inline text, sitting near the lower bound of comfortable legibility for a teaching value at this frame size.
  • [A1 · low] There is a comparatively large empty band between the title block and the first body line, leaving the lower-third of the frame (below ~y=410) sparse while the gap above feels loose; balance is slightly top-heavy.
  • [A7 · low] Both math lines read at similar visual weight; the storyboard marks math.1 with anim: highlight as the punchline (the collision that is 'the whole obstacle'), but on this final static frame it is not strongly more salient than math.0, so the focal collision example does not clearly dominate.

02 · one_to_one_definition definition_math V clean

A1 84A2 90A3 94A4 91A5 93A6 90A7 86
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A1 · low] The two formulas sit low-center while the prose sits high-left, leaving a generous empty mid-frame gap and a bottom-light balance. Within house-style dark-flat minimalism this is acceptable, but the composition is slightly top-heavy on text / center-low on math rather than evenly distributed.
  • [A7 · low] The highlighted equivalent form (math.1, amber) is the one the beat says to 'reach for in a proof', but it is the same point size as math.0, so focus is carried almost entirely by color. Hierarchy reads correctly but leans on a single channel.
  • [A6 · low] Each formula is independently centered on its own width, so their internal anchors (the f(x_1) terms) do not vertically align. Standard centered-display behavior and fully legible, but a shared left or '=' alignment would look more deliberate for two parallel statements.

03 · student_id_is_one_to_one definition_math V clean

A1 84A2 82A3 90A4 80A5 90A6 78A7 83
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A6 · med] The two teaching math lines are centered while the statement prose directly above them ('A class is assigned ID numbers…', left-aligned, starting x≈130) is left-aligned, so the block lacks a single consistent left edge. They read as display-math one-liners (rubric tolerates centered short callouts), hence advisory not blocking, but the mixed alignment slightly weakens the visual grid.
    → Left-align the two math lines to the same left margin as the statement prose (x≈130), or indent them one step, so statement + math.0 + math.1 share a common left edge as one example block.
  • [A1 · low] Large empty mid-frame gap between the title/eyebrow cluster at top and the statement, leaving the upper-middle of the frame unused while content sits low-center; balance is slightly top-heavy then sparse.
  • [A7 · low] math.1 is the highlighted payoff line ('— one-to-one'), but on this final frame its emphasis over the rest of the block is carried mainly by color; the eye is drawn adequately but the 'one-to-one' verdict phrase is not visually distinguished from the rest of math.1's run.

04 · testing_x_and_x_squared example_walkthrough V clean

A1 88A2 86A3 90A4 91A5 89A6 87A7 82
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A7 · med] The accent/focus weight is spread fairly evenly across the three right-column math statements; the 'bad' verdict (the punchline this beat lands on, per say '{show math.2} ... exactly the collision the definition rules out') is not visually elevated above the earlier scaffolding lines beyond its red color. Earlier scaffold (the 'f is one-to-one ✓' line) does not recede once the argument moves to g.
    → On the fullest frame, let the final 'g is not one-to-one ✗' carry slightly more weight (size or a subtle accent panel) and dim the already-resolved 'f is one-to-one ✓' line so the eye lands on the collision verdict the beat is teaching.
  • [A1 · low] The right column's content clusters in the upper-right while the lower-right quadrant is empty, leaving the right half slightly top-heavy against the evenly-distributed left column. Not crowding or overlap — purely a balance nicety.
  • [A6 · low] The takeaway wraps to a second short line ('later.') producing a brief widow word; sentence-level prose is correctly left-aligned, so this is cosmetic only.

05 · shape_can_mislead example_walkthrough V clean

A1 84A2 88A3 92A4 90A5 91A6 80A7 82
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A6 · med] The three right-hand math fragments ('h(x)=x(x-2)(x+2)', 'h is not one-to-one ×', 'x_1=x_2, k one-to-one ✓') are vertically centered within their own column rather than baseline-aligned to the numbered steps they correspond to. Row 02's verdict 'h is not one-to-one ×' floats roughly midway between left rows 01 and 02, and row 03's '✓' line sits below its matching step text, so the eye cannot lock a clean horizontal correspondence between each step and its conclusion.
    → Anchor each right-column callout to the baseline (or vertical center) of its matching left-column step so step 01↔factoring, step 02↔'h not one-to-one ×', step 03↔'k one-to-one ✓' read across on shared rows.
  • [A1 · low] Vertical spacing between the three steps is slightly uneven once the two-line wraps (step 02 '0 ≠ 2.' and step 03 'forces x_1^3 = x_2^3.') are accounted for; the gap above step 03 reads a touch tighter than the gap above step 02, mildly weakening the rhythm of the numbered list.
  • [A7 · low] The two verdict lines carry the beat's punchline (opposite verdicts), but at this fullest frame all three callouts share similar weight; the contrasting × / ✓ pair could be given marginally stronger focus so the 'opposite verdicts' payoff pops over the scaffold factoring line.

06 · horizontal_line_test graph_compare V clean

A1 86A2 88A3 90A4 84A5 88A6 82A7 87
5 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [V8 Advisory] Right panel parabola is plotted as 1.15*x**2 (per payload right.plots[0].expression) but labeled '$y=x^2$'. The two filled crossing dots sit at x=+/-1.043 where the dashed line y=1.25 meets the DRAWN curve 1.15*x^2 (sqrt(1.25/1.15)=1.0425), so they are correctly placed for the curve that is actually rendered.
    → No change needed for blocking. If exact fidelity to '$y=x^2$' is desired, set expression to 'x**2' and re-derive the dashed-line height / crossing points (e.g. line at y=1.3 -> x=+/-1.14); otherwise leave as a declared schematic.
  • [A1 · low] Left dashed line terminates at the single crossing dot near x=1 (start [-2.0,1.0] end [1.3,1.0]), while the right dashed line spans nearly the full panel width (start [-1.95,1.25] end [1.95,1.25]). The two panels read slightly asymmetric.
  • [A4 · low] Same asymmetry as above creates a minor inconsistency in how the 'sweep the horizontal line' motif is presented across the two panels.
  • [A6 · low] Caption wraps to two lines with 'height.' alone on the second line (mild widow). Both captions are center-aligned.
  • [A1 · low] A small brand/audio-waveform mark sits in the lower-right corner, slightly detached from the content grid and competing a little with the annotation line above it.

07 · inverse_function_definition definition_math V clean

A1 88A2 88A3 90A4 90A5 90A6 89A7 89
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A1 · low] There is a noticeably large empty gap between the statement prose block and the display-math block, leaving the centre of the frame under-used while content clusters top and middle. Not information loss, purely whitespace distribution.
  • [A1 · low] The two display equations are independently centred rather than sharing a common optical anchor; the shorter caution sits centred under a longer biconditional, so their left edges do not align and the pair reads slightly loose.
  • [A4 · low] Minor: the caution equation uses the secondary blue while the eyebrow [ DEFINITION ] also uses a similar blue; with the gold accent reserved for the highlighted defining relation this is consistent, but the blue caution does not visually read as a 'warning' the say-text frames it as.

08 · inverse_iff_one_to_one theorem_proof V clean

A1 80A2 84A3 90A4 88A5 88A6 90A7 82
4 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [V4 Advisory] All on-screen Tex renders cleanly: statement 'A function has an inverse if and only if it is one-to-one.'; proof.0 'If f has an inverse and f(x1)=f(x2), apply f^{-1}: x1=x2. So f is one-to-one.'; proof.1 'If f is one-to-one, each output y has exactly one source x — send y back to it and that is f^{-1}.'; qed 'So one-to-one and invertible name the same thing.' Subscripts x_1/x_2 and superscripts f^{-1} are present and legible; no raw $, no stray backslashes, no missing-glyph boxes, no overflow.
    → None required.
  • [A1 · low] Element layout is a little top-weighted — content clusters in the upper two-thirds, leaving a noticeable dead zone bottom-right beyond the faint decorative glyph.
  • [A7 · low] Focus is well-handled overall, but the central theorem statement (the load-bearing 'iff' claim) and the qed line compete slightly for the accent emphasis; the statement could read marginally more prominent than the two-direction proof scaffold.
  • [A2 · low] Inherently text-forward slide (theorem + word proof has no graph), so it leans toward a flat lecture-slide look more than the graph scenes in this deck.

09 · first_inverses example_walkthrough V clean

A1 88A2 87A3 92A4 90A5 93A6 89A7 84
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A1 · low] Largest empty gap on the frame sits between the last step and the takeaway, leaving the composition makes the layout read slightly bottom-light/top-heavy; the three step rows are clustered in the upper-middle while the lower third is nearly empty apart from the takeaway.
  • [A7 · low] On this fullest frame all three math lines carry equal accent weight; the load-bearing new result of each beat (the cube root, then the verification = x with the check) is not visually distinguished from earlier scaffold, so the eye is not led to the current teaching point.
  • [A6 · low] The takeaway is centered while the step prose is left-aligned; this is permissible (short callout/caption may center per rubric), but the centered single line reads slightly detached from the left-aligned column rhythm above it.

10 · composition_identities definition_math V clean

A1 84A2 88A3 92A4 92A5 90A6 78A7 90
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A6 · med] This is a full sentence-level teaching line rendered centered. Per A6, sentence-level prose should read left-aligned; centering is reserved for short title cards/eyebrows/captions. It loses no information (hence A-side, not V-blocking) but reads as a centered prose orphan rather than a left-aligned lead.
    → Left-align the statement to the same x-origin (~160) as the eyebrow and title so the title-block + statement form one left-aligned column; keep only the eyebrow [ PROPOSITION ] centered/short if desired. If the template intends it as a short tagline-style epigraph, that is a borderline keep — flag for author judgment.
  • [A1 · low] Content sits in the upper-middle with a noticeable gap between the title block and the statement, and the lower third is sparse. Balance is slightly top-loaded. This is generous whitespace (house style dark-flat is fine, not a defect by itself), only a mild distribution nit.
  • [A1 · low] Mixed alignment axis: left-aligned title/eyebrow vs center-aligned statement and math creates two competing vertical anchors rather than one consistent grid.

11 · reflection_across_y_equals_x graph_focus V clean

A1 82A2 90A3 93A4 89A5 91A6 92A7 88
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A4 · med] The label is rendered in a red/pink tint, but its corresponding line (storyboard color_role: muted) draws as muted gray — label color is inconsistent with its own line's role and with the muted intent declared in the payload (plot.0).
    → Color the 'y=x' label to match its muted line (gray) so axis-of-reflection scaffolding reads as muted background, not an accent; reserve the warm/accent hue for the inverse curve (cube root) which is the beat's focus.
  • [A1 · low] The dashed y=x line passes through both nearby curve labels, crossing glyphs; the two labels also sit close together in the same horizontal band, slightly crowding the top-right of the plot.
  • [A7 · low] The orange cube-root (inverse, the new reveal this beat) and the blue x^3 read at similar visual weight; the muted axis-of-reflection (y=x) competes slightly because its label is the most saturated (red) element near the curves.

12 · repair_by_restricting graph_focus V clean

A1 84A2 88A3 90A4 90A5 89A6 86A7 80
3 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A4 · med] The 'y = x' label renders in a warm red/pink tint, but plot.0 declares color_role: muted (grey, like the dashed line it labels). The label color drifts from the line's declared role, breaking the accent/role consistency with the actual y=x guide line (which is correctly grey).
    → Make the y=x label inherit the muted role of its line so label and dashed line read as one element; reserve the red/accent tint for elements the beat actually wants to highlight.
  • [A1 · low] The dashed line crosses through the label glyphs, mildly reducing crispness of an otherwise comfortable layout.
  • [A7 · low] The f(x)=x^2 label sits noticeably to the left of where the blue curve passes at its y-height, so the eye must travel to associate label with curve. It is unambiguous (only the blue curve occupies that upper-left region), so this is minor focus polish, not a collision.

13 · finding_the_inverse_strategy procedure_steps V clean

A1 84A2 88A3 92A4 90A5 91A6 85A7 88
5 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [V2 Advisory] Right-hand math column (y=f(x) at ~x=1130, x=… at ~x=1135, y=f^{-1}(x) at ~x=1130) sits far right with a wide empty gutter (~x=760 to ~x=1060) between the left prose and the math; no element overlaps or is occluded.
    → Optionally pull the right-side math column inward (e.g. start math near x=900) so the prose and its math read as one tighter row rather than two far-apart columns.
  • [V5 Advisory] Worked strip uses two horizontal '→' arrows: y=x^2 → x=±√y → 'not a function' (boxed strip, y≈672). Arrows are sequence/flow connectors, not value-bearing markers.
    → No change required; arrows are semantically correct.
  • [A1 · low] Large horizontal gutter between the step prose and its companion math makes each row read as two disconnected columns rather than one phrase; balance leans empty in the middle band.
  • [A6 · low] The worked-example box is inset to the right and centered under the step block instead of aligning to the same left margin as the numbered steps, creating a minor alignment drift in the vertical grid.
  • [A7 · low] The right-column math and the left prose carry similar visual weight; with no single accented focus, the eye is not strongly pulled to the 'current' step (acceptable on a fullest frame, but hierarchy is flatter than ideal).

14 · inverse_of_a_cubic derivation V clean

A1 78A2 84A3 90A4 80A5 92A6 88A7 86
4 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [A1 · med] The equation stack is pushed right and down, leaving a large unbalanced void in the upper-left while content crowds toward center-right. The frame feels off-center rather than using a comfortable visual grid.
    → Left-shift and vertically center the derivation block (or left-align it under the title's left edge) so the negative space is balanced and the equations sit on a consistent left margin shared with the title/eyebrow.
  • [A6 · low] [investigated → FALSE POSITIVE] audit read the deliberately-centered chain block as '=' misaligned; geometric measurement (= left-x equal across all lines) + a zoomed crop confirm the '=' DO form a vertical column. No change.
  • [A4 · low] The two halves of the title differ in font (serif text vs math), size, and weight, and their baselines/optical sizes do not quite agree, so the title reads as two pasted pieces rather than one typographic unit.
  • [A7 · low] The gold highlight on the result line works, but the four scaffold lines are all the same brightness/size, so the eye is not strongly guided through the build order before landing on the result; the scaffold does not visibly recede.

15 · inverse_of_a_rational derivation V clean

A1 70A2 86A3 90A4 78A5 90A6 68A7 74
8 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [V1 Advisory] Title inline formula 'f(x)=3/(x-5)' is rendered oversized; its numerator '3' reaches near the top safe area (~y150 of 1080) and the bottom derivation line 'f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x' sits ~y880. Everything stays inside the frame; nothing is clipped.
    → No action required for correctness; the only adjustment worth making is aesthetic (shrink the oversized title formula, see A6 defect).
  • [V4 Advisory] All Tex renders cleanly: title 'Inverse of f(x)=3/(x-5)', and lines 'y=3/(x-5)', 'x-5=3/y', 'x=5+3/y', 'f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x'. Fraction vincula, the superscript -1, and all digits are intact — no raw '$', no stray backslash, no missing/overflowing glyphs.
    → None — V4 is clean.
  • [V6 Advisory] Frame shows the title formula plus exactly the four payload `lines` (y=3/(x-5); x-5=3/y; x=5+3/y; f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x=(5x+3)/x). The `say` also speaks a both-direction check and a domain-pairing remark, but those are intentionally verbal-only (not in the `lines` payload), so their absence from the frame is expected, not a contradiction.
    → None — V6 is consistent.
  • [V8 Advisory] Visible algebra checks out: y=3/(x-5) -> x-5=3/y -> x=5+3/y -> f^{-1}(x)=5+3/x, and 5+3/x = (5x+3)/x is algebraically identical. Matches the storyboard `lines` verbatim.
    → None — V8 is clean.
  • [A6 · med] Sentence/title-card text and its inline math are baseline-misaligned and size-mismatched, making the title card read as two disjoint pieces rather than one phrase. (A6 wants math and adjacent text baseline-aligned.)
    → Render the title formula at a size matched to 'Inverse of' and vertically center it on the same baseline (or move the whole formula onto a single baseline with the word), so 'Inverse of f(x)=3/(x-5)' reads as one line.
  • [A1 · med] Layout is unbalanced — content hugs the right while the left and the upper-middle are dead space, so the frame does not use a comfortable, evenly-distributed visual grid.
    → Center the derivation column (or left-align it under the title) and tighten the title->first-line gap, distributing the four lines to fill the vertical space more evenly.
  • [A7 · low] The intended focal point (the highlighted result) competes for attention with an over-large title formula; the eye is not cleanly steered to the answer.
  • [A4 · low] The title math is set at a different (larger) scale than the body math, a mild inconsistency in the math type scale across the frame.

16 · inverse_in_an_application derivation V clean

A1 82A2 85A3 88A4 84A5 90A6 88A7 86
4 advisory／polish note(s)
  • [V8 Advisory] Math block, line 2 (counting from top): 't = 9/5 f^{-1}(t) + 32'. This is a non-standard intermediate step — it substitutes f^{-1}(t) into the forward formula's input slot and sets the result equal to t. It renders cleanly and matches storyboard source line.1 verbatim (line 444-446 of ch01_inverse_functions.yml).
    → No render fix required. If the author wants line 2 to read more transparently as a composition setup, consider rewriting source line.1 to 't = f(f^{-1}(t)) = 9/5 f^{-1}(t) + 32'; but this is an authoring/content choice, not a visual blocker — leave as-is unless the narration audit (NFA) flags it.
  • [A6 · low] [investigated → FALSE POSITIVE] audit read the deliberately-centered chain block as '=' misaligned; geometric measurement (= left-x equal across all lines) + a zoomed crop confirm the '=' DO form a vertical column. No change.
  • [A1 · low] The four equations sit low-center, leaving a large empty band between the title/eyebrow (top ~12%) and the first equation (~44% down). The bottom third below the last line is also mostly empty save the logo.
  • [A4 · low] Line 4 'f^{-1}(f(t)) = ... = t' is rendered in the same blue as the scaffold lines, so the closing check reads as ordinary scaffold rather than as the confirming payoff of the derivation.

17 · recap recap_cards V clean

  • [A1 · low] 4-row left column vs 3-card right column slightly imbalanced
  • [A4 · low] three cards differ slightly in height/internal padding
